title: ajuga vulgaris var. stoloniflora
intro: Ajuga vulgaris var. stoloniflora, commonly known as bugleweed or Ajuga, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, which have been used to treat conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and hypertension. The plant is rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Bugleweed can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
